topic: A person finds out they were adopted through a DNA test
raw_output:
[story 1]

The printed report lay crumpled in my hand, the glossy paper mocking me with its sterile perfection. Every line, every percentage, screamed a betrayal. I stared at the screen, my knuckles white where I gripped the edge of the desk. The air in the room felt suddenly thin, suffocating. How could they? After all these years of carefully constructed narratives, of believing the story they fed me? A low, ragged sound escaped my throat, a sound more animal than human. I shoved the computer chair back, the scrape of metal against the hardwood floor echoing loudly in the silence. I needed air. I needed to move, to do anything other than sit here and let the cold, hard facts dismantle everything I thought I knew about my own origins.

[story 2]

My hands were shaking so violently I had to press them against my temples, trying to physically contain the internal tremor. The DNA results were undeniable, a stark, unyielding truth laid bare before me. A hot, tight pressure built behind my eyes, making the room seem too bright, too loud. I walked to the window, the city sprawling beneath me a meaningless tapestry of lights. I clenched my jaw so hard I tasted copper. Every carefully rehearsed defense, every moment of quiet acceptance I had practiced in my head, dissolved into ash. I turned back, my gaze sharp and cold, directed at the empty space where I assumed my family should have been. "This is a lie," I whispered, the sound raspy and laced with a dangerous edge.

[story 3]

I reread the summary three times, each repetition a fresh stab of icy resentment. My breath hitched, catching painfully in my chest. A sudden, intense wave of heat rushed through me, making my skin prickle. I slammed the report onto the mahogany surface; the sound cracked through the quiet of the study. I stood up abruptly, pacing the length of the room like a caged animal. My movements were jerky, uncontrolled. I wanted to scream, to tear the evidence to shreds, but the words caught, trapped behind a wall of pure, simmering indignation. I ran a hand through my hair, pulling at the strands until my fingers ached, unable to settle on anything but the raw, churning anger inside.
